## What this PR does

<!-- One or two sentences. -->

## Type

- [ ] 🧪 New project brief (`projects/<slug>.md`)
- [ ] ✍️ Improvement to an existing brief (milestones / evals / deploy / stack)
- [ ] 📚 New / updated reference resource(s)
- [ ] 🎯 Presentation-checklist upgrade
- [ ] 🐛 Fix (broken link, wrong stack, stale fact, mislabeled license/stars)

## Quality checklist (required)

- [ ] **Earns its slot** — for a new brief: it proves a distinct in-demand skill not already covered, and I said which role it impresses.
- [ ] **Has evals** — the brief names concrete metrics + a target (not "it works").
- [ ] **Has a deploy path** — a concrete recipe to a live URL.
- [ ] **Follows the structure** — Problem · Who it impresses · Skills · Stack · Milestones · Evals · Deploy · Stretch · Reference resources.
- [ ] **Links** — every new link is annotated (one line *why*) and typed (📄 📘 📰 🎬 🧑‍🏫 🛠️ 💻).
- [ ] **Repos** — new repo links note license + approx stars.
- [ ] **License** — any adapted text/code is MIT/Apache/CC0 and attributed; non-commercial/CC-BY-SA is link-only (re-implemented, not copied).
- [ ] **Dated** — facts reflect 2026; I didn't ship anything I couldn't check.
- [ ] **Style** — answer-first, tables over prose, numbers over adjectives, senior voice.
- [ ] I ran a quick self-check that internal links resolve.
